note: The same four future requirements thrown at both skeletons. Hypothesis under test is the TRADEOFF STRUCTURE, not a winner: isolation should pay on cross-cutting change (f4), and cost overhead on simple additions (f1). If one skeleton wins everywhere, publish that.

features.task: Add the ability to fetch a user by their email address, exposed as route 'GET /users/by-email/:email'.
Add an in-memory cache for user reads by id with a 60-second TTL. Cache invalidation on delete.
Replace hard deletion with soft deletion: add a deleted_at timestamp instead of removing rows, and make every read exclude soft-deleted users.
The db client library is being replaced: db.query(sql, params) no longer exists; the new API is db.execute({ sql, params }) which returns { rows }. Migrate the codebase to the new API.
